summary: Chris Riccomini interviews Frederic Branczyk, founder of Polar Signals, about their always-on, zero-instrumentation profiler. The discussion covers Polar Signals's architecture, particularly Parca and FrostDB. Parca achieves minimal invasiveness by using an eBPF filter that samples the entire OS stack at 19hz. The collected data is stored in FrostDB, an embedded storage engine built on Datafusion and Parquet. The interview also references several influential papers on profiling and optimization from Google.
key_points: Polar Signals offers always-on, zero-instrumentation profiling through Parca
Parca uses eBPF filters to sample the entire OS stack at 19hz for minimal invasiveness
FrostDB is an embedded storage engine built on Datafusion and Parquet
The architecture draws inspiration from Google's continuous profiling research
money_quotes: Parca is able to achieve its minimally invasive profiling claims by using an eBPF filter that samples the entire OS's stack at 19hz
FrostDB, an embedded storage engine built on Datafusion and Parquet
key_insight: Modern continuous profiling can be achieved with minimal overhead by combining eBPF sampling with columnar storage engines built on Datafusion and Parquet.
